seoTitle: Make Printable Labels in Google Docs Using Tables
metaDescription: Design mailing, shipping, or product labels in Google Docs by building a table grid matched to your label sheet, then hiding the borders for printing.
intro: You don't need dedicated label software to print mailing or product labels—Google Docs' table tool can be shaped to match standard label sheets. This guide covers setting up your page margins, building the grid, and formatting text so your labels print cleanly.

steps.heading: Adjust page margins to fit your label sheet
steps.body: Use Page Setup to set margins that match the dimensions of your physical label sheet.

steps.heading: Insert a table grid
steps.body: Use the Table tool from the Insert menu to build a grid that maps to your label sheet's layout.

steps.heading: Set exact cell dimensions
steps.body: Adjust individual cell heights and widths through Table Properties for precise alignment with your printable sheet.

steps.heading: Center your text with padding
steps.body: Apply cell padding and text alignment settings so your data sits centered and readable within each label.

steps.heading: Remove borders before printing
steps.body: Set border line widths to zero points so the grid outlines disappear, leaving a clean, print-ready layout.

faq.question: Can I print labels directly from Google Docs?
faq.answer: Yes, once your table grid matches your label sheet dimensions and the borders are hidden, you can print directly from the document.

faq.question: How do I match my table to a specific label sheet?
faq.answer: Adjust your page margins and individual cell width and height values in Table Properties to align with your physical sheet's grid.

faq.question: Why should I remove the table borders?
faq.answer: Setting border widths to zero points removes the visual grid lines so only your text prints on the labels.

faq.question: Is this good for large batches of shipping labels?
faq.answer: Yes, once the grid is set up correctly, it's straightforward to fill in addresses or product names across many cells at once.
